steps.label: cause
steps.text: Old Garnacha and Carinena vines grow on steep, low-fertility, freely draining licorella slopes in a warm, dry region.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Limited water and nutrient availability restrict vigour and yields, while strong sunlight supports ripening; deep roots help vines access scarce water.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Small crops of ripe grapes can produce deeply flavoured, concentrated and structured red wines.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: low-yielding vines on dry, low-fertility licorella slopes help explain Priorat's concentration.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
